protocol.name: tawc_gfxstream
protocol.description:
Custom Vulkan WSI hand-off between a chroot client using libvulkan_gfxstream.so (kumquat-backed) and the tawc compositor.
The chroot's custom Vulkan WSI allocates swapchain images as gfxstream ColorBuffers via the kumquat protocol's RESOURCE_CREATE_BLOB; the resulting u32 resource_id is the same `HandleType` that gfxstream's host-side FrameBuffer uses to index its color-buffer table.
On vkQueuePresentKHR the chroot ships only that id to the compositor here. The compositor (same process as the gfxstream host renderer) looks the id up via FrameBuffer::exportColorBuffer to obtain the backing AHardwareBuffer*, then drives it through the existing AHB-to-GLES-texture import path (the same path the libhybris android_wlegl backend uses).
No fds are exchanged: the AHB already lives in the compositor's address space, and swapchain images are DEVICE_LOCAL so the chroot never needs CPU access. See notes/gfxstream-bridge.md "WSI plan: custom Vulkan WSI".

interface.name: tawc_gfxstream

description.summary: bind gfxstream ColorBuffer resources as wl_buffers

interface.description: Singleton global. Bound by the chroot's custom Vulkan WSI; not used by the libhybris path (which goes via android_wlegl instead).

entry.name: unknown_colorbuffer
entry.summary: colorbuffer_id is not present in gfxstream's FrameBuffer

entry.name: not_ahb_backed
entry.summary: colorbuffer_id resolved, but no AHardwareBuffer is attached (this should not happen for swapchain-image allocations on Android — gfxstream's external-memory path always uses AHBs)

request.name: create_buffer

description.summary: bind a gfxstream ColorBuffer as a wl_buffer

request.description:
Create a wl_buffer backed by an existing gfxstream ColorBuffer identified by `colorbuffer_id`. The compositor acquires the underlying AHardwareBuffer and releases it on wl_buffer destruction.
Width, height, and format are advisory metadata used to size and configure the GLES texture import. They must match the ColorBuffer's actual dimensions and DRM_FOURCC encoding — a mismatch is a client bug.
Errors: unknown_colorbuffer — `colorbuffer_id` is not currently registered with gfxstream's FrameBuffer. The client failed to allocate it, or already destroyed it. not_ahb_backed — the ColorBuffer exists but has no AHB backing. Shouldn't happen on Android with our build of gfxstream.

request.name: destroy
request.description: The global itself is stateless; existing wl_buffers stay alive until they are destroyed.
